title: tulipa sylvestris subsp. cuspidata
intro: Tulipa sylvestris subsp. cuspidata, also known as the wild tulip or wolf's bane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family, characterized by its cup-shaped flowers and dark red to almost black petals. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its bulbs, which contain alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Tulipa sylvestris subsp. cuspidata include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and topical creams for various health concerns.
